violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC given CFSM application at the time of inspection. Per PIC, staff are registered to take a food safety manager course on 6/17/25. PIC to send proof of course completion/CFSM certificate to inspector. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/25/2025

The hood ventilation system has an accumulation of dust/grease. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary. Per PIC, hood system is scheduled for a cleaning. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for Food Protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC given CFSM application at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food artichokes with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The following was observed: 1) Shellstock in the walk-in cooler and at the cookline were observed without shellfish tags 2) The shellstock tags are not dated when the last shellstock is used. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw chicken stored above raw squid in fryolator prep unit). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Chemicals were stored on the same shelf with food/equipment/single service articles under the prep table and rolling carts. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged octopus labeled "KEEP FROZEN" was observed thawing with the package in-tact. Reduced oxygen packaged seafood must be removed from the package upon thawing. Food observed at 35 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC removed food from packaging. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The non-food contact surfaces were observed with: 1) The hood ventilation system has an accumulation of dust/grease 2) The ice machine was observed with pink residue on the ice guard The non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned as often as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
